name: Tesseract OCR
summary: Tesseract OCR is Google's open-source optical character recognition engine, using LSTM-based neural networks since version 4 for line recognition. It is primarily accessed through the pytesseract Python wrapper, which calls the underlying system binary.
use_when: High-quality scanned documents, PDFs, and clean digital images with uniform text. Custom font training workflows and environments requiring a fully open-source OCR engine.
avoid_when: Natural scene images, mobile captures with complex backgrounds, or low-resolution photos without preprocessing. Pure Python environments where installing system binaries is not permitted.
pros: Battle-tested engine with decades of development and widespread community support
Completely free and open-source under Apache 2.0 license
Trainable on custom fonts and languages via .traineddata files
Fast CPU inference on clean, high-contrast document images with minimal latency
cons: Poor accuracy on natural scene text, photographs, and low-contrast images without heavy preprocessing
Highly sensitive to image quality, requiring deskewing, binarization, and noise removal
Requires OS-level installation of the tesseract binary in addition to the Python wrapper
Not an end-to-end Python solution; pytesseract is only a thin wrapper around CLI subprocess calls

key_hyperparams.name: lang
key_hyperparams.note: Language model traineddata to use. 'eng' for English, 'eng+deu' for multi-language. Must match installed .traineddata files on the system.

key_hyperparams.name: config
key_hyperparams.note: Tesseract command-line config string. Use '--psm 3' for auto page segmentation, '--psm 6' for single uniform text block, '--psm 11' for sparse text. '--oem 3' uses LSTM engine.

key_hyperparams.name: nice
key_hyperparams.note: Process priority modifier. 0 is default. Positive values lower priority, negative values require elevated permissions.

quick_start:
from PIL import Image
import pytesseract

img = Image.open('document.png')
text = pytesseract.image_to_string(
    img, lang='eng', config='--psm 6 --oem 3'
)
print(text)
decision_notes: Choose Tesseract for custom font training and fully open-source OCR. Use EasyOCR for multi-language support with simpler API.
